steps.title: Prepare Acrylamide/Bis-acrylamide Stock Solution
steps.instruction: 1.1. Dissolve 29 g of acrylamide and 1 g of bis-acrylamide in about 60 mL of distilled water. 1.2. Add water until the total volume reaches 100 mL. Note: Store at 4°C (protect from light).

steps.title: Prepare APS Stock Solution
steps.instruction: Dissolve 0.033g APS in 330 µl of distilled water Note: Store at 4°C (prepare fresh weekly).

steps.title: Prepare Gel Solution
steps.instruction: 3.1. Take 12 mL of distilled water in a clean glass beaker. 3.2. Add 4 mL 30% Polpolyacrylamidelution to it. 3.3. Add 4 mL of 5X TBE buffer. 3.2. Stir gently until the mixture is homogeneous.

steps.title: Add Initiators and Mix
steps.instruction: 4.1. Add 330 µl of 10% APS while stirring 4.2. At last, add 16.7 µl TEMED and mix quickly. Note: APS and TEMED are highly reactive. Therefore, we should always add them last, just seconds before pouring the liquid into the glass plates (keep stirring).

steps.title: Pour and Polymerize Gel
steps.instruction: 5.1. Immediately pour the gel solution between gel plates (polymerisation begins within seconds) 5.2. Insert the comb carefully to form wells. 5.3. Allow the gel to polymerise (approximately 30-35 min) at room temperature. 5.4. Remove the comb once the gel is fully solidified. 5.5. Rinse wells gently with buffer using a pipette to remove trapped air bubbles.

steps.title: Pre-run Gel and Load Samples
steps.instruction: 6.1. Pre-run the gel (without samples) in 1X TBE at 120 V for 5 minutes. 6.2. Load samples into wells. 6.3. Run the gel at 120 V until the dye front reaches the bottom or runs off the gel (around 50-55 min).

steps.title: Stain and Visualize Gel
steps.instruction: 7.1. Stain in a 0.5% ethidium bromide solution for 15 min. 7.2. Rinse briefly with distilled water to enhance contrast. 7.3. Visualise the gel under a UV transilluminator.
